metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4635
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 2. Confocal immunofluorescence staining of cultured hippocampal neurons demonstrating that Vezatin was localized in neurons positive for the neuronal marker Tubb3 and colocalized with the excitatory postsynaptic marker PSD-95 but not with the excitatory presynaptic marker synapsin 1.

further functional studies show that a knockdown of Vezatin affects GluA1 phosphorylation via PKA (suggestive of postsynaptic localization rather than presynaptic, which supports the observations from Figure 2). Experimental description: Immunofluorescence staining was performed on cultures hippocampal neurons from mice. M&M info on Immunofluorescence staining of human brain tissue reflects methods for Figure 7.

"Immunofluorescence staining
The immunofluorescence procedures were described previously [20, 28]. The mice were rapidly euthanized, and mouse brain tissues were fixed with 4% paraformaldehyde for 24 h; the human brain tissues were also fixed with 4% paraformaldehyde for 24 h."

"In cultured hippocampal neurons, immunofluorescence staining showed that vezatin (green) was expressed in neurons positive for the neuronal marker beta-Tubulin III (Tubb3) (red) and colocalized with the excitatory postsynaptic marker postsynaptic density protein 95 (PSD-95) (purple) (Fig. 2a) but not with the presynaptic marker synapsin 1 (purple) (Fig. 2b). "

Antibodies described in this annotation;
mouse vezatin antibody (Santa Cruz Biotechnology; 1:100)
mouse Tubb3 antibody (Proteintech; 1:400)
rabbit PSD-95 antibody (Abcam; 1:200)
rabbit Synapsin 1 antibody (Novus; 1:200)
